prerequisites: A 100ms application (workspace) created in the 100ms dashboard.
The app access key and app secret for that application, available from the Developer section of the 100ms dashboard, which are used to generate the management token.
A start_date value to bound the data the connector reads.

authMethods.name: Management token
authMethods.summary: 100ms authenticates its server-side REST and management APIs with a management token, a JWT generated from your app access key and app secret. The connector's secret field management_token holds this token. The token is not meant to be exposed on the client side. It carries the access credentials, a token type of management, and an expiry (default 7 days, with a maximum validity of 14 days). For testing, a pre-generated management token is also available in the Developer section of the 100ms dashboard.

setupSteps.title: Get your app credentials
setupSteps.body: Sign in to the 100ms dashboard and open the Developer section for your application. Copy the app access key and app secret. These are the credentials used to generate a management token.

setupSteps.title: Obtain a management token
setupSteps.body: Generate a management token from the app access key and app secret. For testing you can copy a pre-generated management token directly from the Developer section of the dashboard. For production, generate tokens yourself from the credentials. The token authenticates calls to the 100ms server-side REST APIs and should be treated as a secret and never exposed client-side.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Set management_token to the management token you obtained, and set start_date to the date from which the connector should begin reading data. Both fields are required.
